heading: Enforcement of Senate subpena or order
label: 288d Enforcement of Senate subpena or order
notes: CodificationSubsec. (f) of this section amended title 28 by adding section 1364 and by adding item 1364 to the chapter analysis.
Amendments1986—Subsec. (a). substituted “” for “”.Pub. L. 99–336section 1365 of title 28section 1364 of title 28

children.heading: Institution of civil actions
children.label: a Institution of civil actions
children.text: When directed to do so pursuant to , the Counsel shall bring a civil action under any statute conferring jurisdiction on any court of the United States (including ), to enforce, to secure a declaratory judgment concerning the validity of, or to prevent a threatened failure or refusal to comply with, any subpena or order issued by the Senate or a committee or a subcommittee of the Senate authorized to issue a subpena or order.section 288b(b) of this titlesection 1365 of title 28

children.heading: Actions in name of committees and subcommittees
children.label: b Actions in name of committees and subcommittees
children.text: Any directive to the Counsel to bring a civil action pursuant to subsection (a) in the name of a committee or subcommittee of the Senate shall, for such committee or subcommittee, constitute authorization to bring such action within the meaning of any statute conferring jurisdiction on any court of the United States.

children.heading: Consideration of resolutions authorizing actions
children.label: c Consideration of resolutions authorizing actions

children.text: such resolution is reported by a majority of the members voting, a majority being present, of such committee or committee of which such subcommittee is a subcommittee, and

children.label: A
children.text: the procedure followed in issuing such subpena;

children.label: B
children.text: the extent to which the party sub­penaed has complied with such subpena;

children.label: C
children.text: any objections or privileges raised by the subpenaed party; and

children.label: D
children.text: the comparative effectiveness of bringing a civil action under this section, certification of a criminal action for contempt of Congress, and initiating a contempt proceeding before the Senate.

as an exercise of the rulemaking power of the Senate, and, as such, they shall be considered as part of the rules of the Senate, and such rules shall supersede any other rule of the Senate only to the extent that rule is inconsistent therewith; and

with full recognition of the constitutional right of the Senate to change such rules (so far as relating to the procedure in the Senate) at any time, in the same manner, and to the same extent as in the case of any other rule of the Senate.

children.heading: Committee reports
children.label: e Committee reports
children.text: A report filed pursuant to subsection (c)(2) shall not be receivable in any court of law to the extent such report is in compliance with such subsection.

children.heading: Certification of failure to testify; contempt
children.label: g Certification of failure to testify; contempt

children.text: the President pro tempore of the Senate in certifying to the United States Attorney for the District of Columbia any matter pursuant to ; orsection 194 of this title
the Senate to hold any individual or entity in contempt of the Senate.
